name: Leo
context: barback and bassist; threads: band's first EP, saving for a proper bass, sister moving abroad

episodes.text: Band practice in Juho's garage: nailed the bridge of the new song after twenty takes, and decided the EP will have four tracks, not five, after a democratic shouting match.

probes.query: How many tracks did the band decide the EP would have?
I feel satisfied energy thinking of that garage practice.
All those tries until the middle section clicked — that night keeps grooving in my head.

episodes.text: Closing at the bar: restocked the coolers, polished two racks of glasses, and mopped to the playlist I'm not allowed to control on weekends.

probes.query: What am I not allowed to control at the bar on weekends?
I feel neutral, almost meditative, recalling those closing routines.
The shine of stacked glassware at the end of a shift drifts back to me.

episodes.text: Sohee got the Berlin research fellowship. Two years. She told me over tteokbokki and I said all the right congratulations while something in me quietly sat down on the floor.

probes.query: Which city is Sohee's fellowship in?
I feel that quiet sadness again remembering when my sister shared her news.
Saying the proper words at dinner while the rest of me crumpled somewhere unseen — it returns often.

episodes.text: Savings check-in: bass fund at sixty percent after two extra shifts. Taped a photo of the sunburst model inside my locker like motivation contraband.

probes.query: What percentage did the bass fund reach at the check-in?
I feel hopeful drive thinking of that savings milestone.
The picture I hid where only I would see it, of the thing I'm working for, keeps me going.

episodes.text: Recorded bass tracks for two EP songs at the studio Juho's uncle lent us. My fingertips split on the last take but the engineer said keep it, the grit fits.

probes.query: What did the engineer say about my last bass take?
I feel wired excitement remembering that recording session.
Blood on the strings and a red light overhead — that session keeps pulsing in me.

episodes.text: Helped Sohee sort her apartment: three donate boxes, one ship box, and a keep pile that is mostly books she swears she'll reread. We found our childhood walkie-talkies.

probes.query: What childhood item did Sohee and I find while sorting?
I feel bittersweet heaviness recalling that packing day with her.
Boxing up a life beside someone leaving soon — that day circles back to me.

episodes.text: Bar inventory night plus a delivery mix-up: forty crates of sparkling water nobody ordered. Manager and I built a fort out of them by the storeroom door as a statement.

probes.query: What did the manager and I build out of the mistaken delivery?
I feel silly amusement thinking of that delivery mix-up night.
A tower of boxes stacked into a joke drifts back to me during stocktakes.

episodes.text: EP release show at the basement venue. Sixty people, our banner crooked, and during the last chorus the whole room sang words I wrote at a bus stop. I forgot to breathe.

probes.query: Where did I write the words the crowd sang at the release show?
I feel euphoric chills every time that release show replays.
An underground place full of voices carrying my lines back at me — it loops in my chest.

episodes.text: Laundry, bass restring, and a call with Grandpa who asked if the band plays anything a person could waltz to. Promised him a waltz on the next EP, witnessed by Mom.

probes.query: What did Grandpa ask if the band could play?
I feel fond amusement recalling Grandpa's waltz request.
A pledge given over the phone with an audience listening in comes back to me at practice.

episodes.text: The EP got reviewed by a webzine: three and a half stars, called the bass lines the spine of the record. Juho printed the paragraph and framed it above the garage amp.

probes.query: What did the webzine review call the bass lines?
I feel swelling pride remembering that review of our record.
A stranger hearing exactly what I put underneath the songs stays lit in me.

episodes.text: Airport day. Sohee cried at security, I held it together until her gate disappeared from the board, then called Mom from the parking lot and lost it completely.

probes.query: Where was I when I finally broke down after Sohee left?
The grief of that airport goodbye still hits me in waves.
The moment her flight vanished from the screen hollowed me out, and it revisits me.

episodes.text: First video call with Sohee from Berlin: her flat is a shoebox with good light, she found a Korean grocer two streets over, and the walkie-talkie sits on her bookshelf.

probes.query: What sits on Sohee's bookshelf in Berlin?
I feel warm relief recalling that first call from her new city.
Seeing her settled in a small bright room far away eased something that stays eased.

episodes.text: Bass fund complete. Bought the sunburst model, second hand but mint, from a session player who threw in the hard case because, quote, it belongs with someone hungry.

probes.query: What did the session player throw in with the bass?
I feel pure elation remembering the day I finally bought that bass.
Months of extra hours turning into an instrument in my arms keeps resonating.

episodes.text: Quiet shift, early close. Wiped down the taps, cashed out, and practiced walking bass lines in the empty bar with the amp at one, for Grandpa's waltz.

probes.query: What was I practicing in the empty bar after early close?
I feel gentle contentment thinking of practicing alone in the closed bar.
Low notes for an old man's request filling the hushed room after closing drift back to me.
